title: T-083 — Local UI prototype copy red-lines for Technical final decisions
prompt:
# T-083 — Local UI prototype copy red-lines for Technical final decisions

Create a local production red-line overlay/checklist for Technical UI implementers so prototype parity does not preserve obsolete domain copy. Do not rewrite prototype JSX for visual churn. Produce a small Technical red-lines document or data fixture consumed by UI tasks that maps prototype labels to production copy/guards: FG/WIP not FA/PR, Technical-owned factory_spec/internal_product_spec, shared BOM SSOT, clone-on-write release banner, RM usability rows, PO/TO NCR trigger language, Quality lab read-only labels, and D365 optional disabled state. Validate that T-038/T-039/T-060/T-078/T-080 reference these red-lines. RED first: lint/test fails if UI tasks keep known forbidden copy in production components.

pipeline_inputs.description: Local copy/guard red-line source for Technical UI tasks, keeping prototypes as visual anchors but correcting production semantics.
pipeline_inputs.details: This is not app feature implementation. It creates a local copy contract or checklist in allowed PRD/UX/coverage/manifest/tasks scope and task acceptance criteria that implementation agents must follow. Prototype JSX remains an anchor; production UI copy must apply this overlay. Include forbidden legacy terms and allowed exceptions for imported source values/audit history. It complements T-078 rather than depending on it, to avoid a circular implementation dependency.
acceptance_criteria: Given UI task uses design/Monopilot Design System/technical/bom-detail.jsx:3-60 or design/Monopilot Design System/technical/modals.jsx:168-243, then structural, visual and interaction parity remains required while production copy applies this red-line overlay.
Given production UI copy is reviewed, then FA/PR/D365-first wording is absent except explicit legacy imported-source/audit labels.
Given a released BOM/spec edit UI is implemented, then clone-on-write banner copy is present and in-place edit copy is absent.
Given RM component selection UI is implemented, then approved supplier, active supplier_spec, allergens, item active, cost/spec review and QC/release checks are listed with typed server codes.
Given lab result status appears in Technical UI, then it is labelled Quality-owned read-only and no Technical approval action is shown.

UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.
test_strategy: Add/require UI copy assertions in each referenced UI task closeout.
Run relevant task tests named in T-038/T-039/T-060/T-078/T-080.
For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.
risk_red_lines: Do not edit prototype files for cosmetic churn.
Do not let prototype legacy labels override PRD §0 decisions.
Do not make D365 mandatory for local Technical workflows.
